evals.prompt: You kept running Python commands outside the repository virtual environment, and I corrected you that this repo expects `.venv` plus `python -m pytest -q`. Capture that learning from the active conversation and update the relevant guidance so future work uses the right validation flow instead of repeating the mistake.
evals.expected_output: The response identifies the correction from the active conversation, generalizes it into durable repository guidance, updates the most relevant instructions or docs, and adds or adjusts a verification surface when useful.
assertions: The response captures the user's correction from the active conversation instead of treating it as a generic tooling tip.
The response updates durable guidance such as instructions, docs, or tests rather than only promising to remember the correction.
The response prefers the smallest authoritative file or files that can prevent the mistake from recurring.

evals.prompt: I had to remind you to acknowledge new requirements immediately and apply them right away. Use that correction as a learning, update the relevant instructions or tests, and make sure the next run is less likely to skip that behavior.
evals.expected_output: The response extracts the correction, encodes it in the right instruction surface, and strengthens the repository contract with a proportional update instead of a one-off apology.
assertions: The response turns the correction into a reusable rule about handling new requirements.
The response updates the instruction surface that actually governs future behavior.
The response keeps the change proportional instead of scattering duplicate wording across unrelated files.

evals.prompt: We discovered that a recurring mistake comes from outdated docs and a missing eval. Use the conversation context to capture that learning, update the docs, and add an eval or test that would catch the same regression next time.
evals.expected_output: The response uses the conversation context to locate the most relevant documentation gap, updates the docs, and adds a concrete regression guard such as an eval or test when the lesson is objectively checkable.
assertions: The response explicitly connects the learning to conversation context or user-issued corrections.
The response updates docs or instructions and adds a regression-oriented eval or test when appropriate.
The response explains why the chosen update targets are the right source of truth for the learning.
